title: How Cambodia Can Fix Its Tourism Problem
gist: Cambodia had its worst tourism year in a decade in 2025 (arrivals down ~17% to 5.57M) and the slide accelerated into 2026 (down 47.8% year-on-year through May), driven by the Thailand border conflict and the country's association with cyber-scam compounds.

insights.claim: Cambodia's international tourist arrivals finished 2025 at 5.57 million, down almost 17 percent year-on-year, with a 43 percent fall in December alone.
insights.speaker: Peter J. Brongers
insights.so_what: Quantifies a severe tourism recession pressuring Cambodia's services economy and FX earnings.

insights.claim: Cambodia's tourism slide worsened in 2026, with arrivals for the first five months down 47.8 percent year-on-year and May, at 232,000, the weakest month yet.
insights.speaker: Peter J. Brongers
insights.so_what: Accelerating decline signals no near-term bottom, a warning for Cambodia-exposed hospitality and consumption.

insights.claim: Cambodia's border conflict with Thailand prompted foreign travel advisories and cancellations across every market, compounding the tourism downturn.
insights.speaker: Peter J. Brongers
insights.so_what: Geopolitical border risk is directly convertible into travel-advisory-driven demand destruction.

insights.claim: Cambodia's association with cyber-scam compounds did real damage in Asian tourist markets, with PM Hun Manet describing the scam operations as a black economy undermining the legitimate one.
insights.speaker: Peter J. Brongers
insights.so_what: The scam-hub reputation is a durable, hard-to-reverse drag on Cambodia's tourism brand and investment climate.
